items.prompt: The area of a circle is A = pi r^2. The rate of change of area with respect to the radius r, dA/dr, when r = 5 is k pi. Find k.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Example 1 (dA/dr = 2 pi r = 10 pi)

items.prompt: A function f is increasing on an interval I if, for all x1 < x2 in I:
On (a, b), a differentiable function f is increasing if:
items.answer: f'(x) >= 0 for each x in (a, b)

expected.correct: f'(x) >= 0 for each x in (a, b)
options: f'(x) >= 0 for each x in (a, b)
f'(x) <= 0 for each x in (a, b)

items.prompt: f has a maximum value in I at a point c if:
items.answer: f(c) > f(x) for all x in I

expected.correct: f(c) > f(x) for all x in I
options: f(c) > f(x) for all x in I
f(c) < f(x) for all x in I

items.prompt: A local maximum value of f at an interior point c means f(c) is:
items.answer: greater than the values of f at all points near c

expected.correct: greater than the values of f at all points near c
options: greater than the values of f at all points near c
greater than f at every point of the domain
the value at an end point

items.prompt: For f(x) = x^3, f'(0) = 0, yet x = 0 is:
items.answer: neither a local maximum nor a local minimum (a point of inflexion)

expected.correct: neither a local maximum nor a local minimum (a point of inflexion)
options: neither a local maximum nor a local minimum (a point of inflexion)

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Remark after Theorem 2 (x^3)

items.prompt: A critical point of f is a point c where:
items.answer: f'(c) = 0 or f is not differentiable at c

expected.correct: f'(c) = 0 or f is not differentiable at c
options: f'(c) = 0 or f is not differentiable at c
c is an end point of the domain

items.prompt: By the first derivative test, a critical point c is a point of local maxima if f'(x) changes:
items.answer: from positive to negative as x increases through c

expected.correct: from positive to negative as x increases through c
options: from positive to negative as x increases through c
from negative to positive as x increases through c

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Theorem 3 (First Derivative Test)

items.prompt: By the second derivative test, x = c is a point of local maxima if f'(c) = 0 and:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Theorem 4 (Second Derivative Test)

items.prompt: State the second derivative test for a twice-differentiable function f at a point c, and say what to do when f''(c) = 0.

expected.rubric: If f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) < 0, then c is a point of local maxima; if f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0, then c is a point of local minima. If f''(c) = 0 the test fails (is inconclusive), and one falls back on the first derivative test to classify c.
markPoints: f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) < 0 gives a local maximum
f'(c) = 0 and f''(c) > 0 gives a local minimum
f''(c) = 0: test fails, use the first derivative test

items.prompt: The absolute maximum value of f on an interval is also called its:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Sec 6.3 absolute maximum

items.prompt: A continuous function on a closed interval [a, b]:
items.answer: attains both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum value

expected.correct: attains both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum value
options: attains both an absolute maximum and an absolute minimum value
need not attain any extreme value
attains a maximum but never a minimum

items.prompt: When finding absolute extrema of f on [a, b], besides the critical points you must also evaluate f at:
items.answer: the end points a and b

expected.correct: the end points a and b
options: the end points a and b

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Sec 6.3 working rule (Steps 1-4)

items.prompt: Describe the working rule for finding the absolute maximum and absolute minimum values of a continuous function f on a closed interval [a, b].

expected.rubric: Step 1: find all critical points of f in [a, b] (where f'(x) = 0 or f is not differentiable). Step 2/3: evaluate f at each critical point and at the end points a and b. Step 4: the greatest of these values is the absolute maximum value and the least is the absolute minimum value.
markPoints: Find the critical points in the interval (f'(x) = 0 or not differentiable)
Evaluate f at the critical points AND the end points a, b
The largest value is the absolute maximum, the smallest the absolute minimum

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch06 Sec 6.3 working rule
